claim: In a retest of items from their first midterm, students showed high performance (70% correct) compared to previous studies, with psychology students (majors and minors) showing better performance than nonpsychology students (81% vs. 69%), d  .74.
note: Verified: reported key t=3.26 matches result_01 exactly. Checked Phase 1 hypothesis column: the article's a priori prediction concerns forgetting-over-time (retest vs original), not the psych-vs-nonpsych comparison actually tested here, so no legitimate pre-specified direction applies -- keep two_sided.
model_formula: 16ItemQuizFollowupPerformance ~ Speciality; Student independent-samples t-test, equal variances
contrast_direction: psychology students minus nonpsychology students; Speciality 1 minus Speciality 0

claim: Students whose scientific memes were publicly shared reported higher levels of perceived learning.
note: RESOLVED -- there is no reporting inconsistency in the article. Table 2 of Clinton-Lisell & Kelly (2023) reports six t-tests; only the perceived-learning value carries a marker (2.79**) and the table note defines '** p < .01'. The Anxiety row reads -0.95 with NO marker, i.e. non-significant, with d = -0.19 and 95% CI [-0.58, 0.20]. The 'p < .01' recorded against this target came from Phase 2 extraction carrying the asterisk down from the row above; it is not in the article. Our recomputation (t(100) = -0.946875, p = .345985, d = -0.187750, public M = 2.030864, closed M = 2.215278) matches Table 2 exactly. Almuhanna marks it 'Fully reproducible'. Moved from pending to ready: Wave 1, equal-variance independent-groups t-test, two-sided published test.
project_verified_result: t(100) = -0.95, d = -0.19, 95% CI [-0.58, 0.20] (Table 2; no significance marker)
model_formula: anxiety ~ control0share1; Student independent-samples t-test, equal variances
contrast_direction: public sharing condition minus closed control condition; control0share1 1 minus 0
extraction_note: Recovered group sizes, t, df, exact p, pooled-SD d, mean difference and SE. Public n = 54; closed n = 48; public mean = 2.030864; closed mean = 2.215278.

claim: Results from the learning outcomes demonstrated increased research knowledge and skills and a positive overall experience with the project.
note: Verified: reported second t=6.15 matches result_02 exactly (df CORRECTED to 28, not 36). Same pre-specified positive direction as claim_01. [Directionality policy: the published significance test for this result is two-sided, so the Bayes factor is computed two-sided to match. The directional Phase 1 hypothesis is recorded but does not change the sidedness of the comparison; only study_20, whose authors ran a genuinely one-sided test, stays one-sided.]
extraction_note: No imputation. Analysis uses pairwise-complete PRE/POST values. The article table reports t and d but does not report df; df follows from the number of complete pairs. Positive signs reflect POST - PRE.

claim: On the final test of knowledge transfer, participants performed equally well on lectured and nonlectured questions.
note: Article reports a ONE-SIDED paired t-test (claim_01 p = .119; claim_02 p = .319). Directionality policy: sidedness follows the original published test, so the one-sided p is retained and compute_wave1_bayes_factors() computes a one-sided (order-restricted) Bayes factor, keeping p and BF on the same footing. Supersedes the earlier decision to convert this p to two-sided.
extraction_note: Recovered paired n, t, df, mean difference, SE and paired dz. The reported p matches one-sided p = 0.119362, not two-sided p = 0.238724. This row is flagged so the Bayesian layer does not silently treat the reported p as two-sided.

claim: Perceived need for reform and number of student researchers supervised/year correlated with teaching these issues, suggesting that those who believe more change is needed in psychology research and are more involved in shaping the next generation of psychology researchers are more likely to discuss replication and reform in their courses.
note: Verified: reported key r=.46 matches result_01 exactly. No explicit directional hypothesis in Phase 1 (aim/RQ substituted) -- keep two_sided.

contrast_direction: positive association between reform and grad_replication
